revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Shinto shrine" to "Infobox religious building"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Ukanomitama no Mikoto

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. {{神社 | 名称=西奈彌羽黒神社 | 画像=[[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|西奈弥羽黒神社]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | 所在地=新潟県村上市羽黒町6-16 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神=奈都比売大神
倉稲魂命
月読命 | 社格=式内社（小）・県社 | 創建=[[持統天皇]]元年（[[687年]]） | 例祭=[[7月7日]] }} '''西奈彌羽黒神社'''（せなみはぐろじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[村上市]]羽黒町にある神社。同市瀬波浜町の[[西奈彌神社]]とともに、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]「越後国磐船郡 西奈彌神社」の論社である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。当地では単に'''羽黒神社'''と呼ばれる。 元々の祭神は奈都比売大神で、その後に[[出羽三山]]から分祀された[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]と[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]も主祭神とされる。この三神をもって江戸時代には羽黒三所権現と称していた。奈津比売神は[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]の孫の[[ハヤマト|羽戸山命]]の子であり、当地を開拓した神であると伝えられる。 == 歴史 == [[持統天皇]]元年（[[687年]]）の創建と伝わっている。元々は西奈彌山の頂上にあったものである。 [[天正]]年間、当地を治めていた[[本庄繁長]]は、出羽庄内の[[大宝寺氏]]を救援するため、[[最上氏]]・東禅寺氏（大宝寺氏家臣）と対戦関係になった。繁長は[[出羽三山]]のひとつ出羽神社に願をかけたところ見事敵をやぶった。このことから繁長は出羽神社の神を分祀して当社に合祀し、当社を総鎮守とした。その後[[村上忠勝]]のとき、社地は西奈弥山頂上からその中腹へと移った。 [[本庄氏]]から[[堀氏]]へと統治者が変っても同じく崇敬された。[[堀直寄]]は[[村上城]]の改築を行い、三層の天守が作られたが、天守の上からは当社を見下ろすことになってしまい恐れ多いとして、[[寛永]]10年（[[1632年]]）、現在地に社殿を造営して遷座した。このときより当社は名を羽黒三所権現と変えた。遷座の日は旧暦6月7日で、以降この日が[[例祭]]日とされ、明治の太陽暦採用以後は月遅れの7月7日とされた。 [[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）に現在の名称に改称され、明治27年（1894年）郷社に、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）には県社に列した。 == 主な年中行事 == * [[7月7日]] - [[村上大祭]] == 現地情報 == 村上市街南部、山居山の中腹に立地する。 ; 交通アクセス * 村上市まちなか循環バス 「羽黒町」バス停より徒歩約3分 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 西奈彌羽黒神社（せなみはぐろじんじゃ）] - 村上市観光協会 * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 にいがた観光ナビ：西奈彌羽黒神社] - 新潟県観光協会 * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html 『羽黒神社とその祭礼』テキスト版] - 岩船広域図書館 - WayBack Machineによるアーカイブ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せなみはくろしんしや}} [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧県社]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:村上市の歴史]] [[Category:羽黒神社]] [[Category:村上市の建築物]]

{{神社 | 名称=西奈彌羽黒神社 | 画像=[[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|西奈弥羽黒神社]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | 所在地=新潟県村上市羽黒町6-16 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | 祭神=奈都比売大神
倉稲魂命
月読命 | 社格=式内社（小）・県社 | 創建=[[持統天皇]]元年（[[687年]]） | 例祭=[[7月7日]] }} '''西奈彌羽黒神社'''（せなみはぐろじんじゃ）は、[[新潟県]][[村上市]]羽黒町にある神社。同市瀬波浜町の[[西奈彌神社]]とともに、[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]「越後国磐船郡 西奈彌神社」の論社である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。当地では単に'''羽黒神社'''と呼ばれる。 元々の祭神は奈都比売大神で、その後に[[出羽三山]]から分祀された[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]と[[ツクヨミ|月読命]]も主祭神とされる。この三神をもって江戸時代には羽黒三所権現と称していた。奈津比売神は[[スサノオ|素盞嗚命]]の孫の[[ハヤマト|羽戸山命]]の子であり、当地を開拓した神であると伝えられる。 == 歴史 == [[持統天皇]]元年（[[687年]]）の創建と伝わっている。元々は西奈彌山の頂上にあったものである。 [[天正]]年間、当地を治めていた[[本庄繁長]]は、出羽庄内の[[大宝寺氏]]を救援するため、[[最上氏]]・東禅寺氏（大宝寺氏家臣）と対戦関係になった。繁長は[[出羽三山]]のひとつ出羽神社に願をかけたところ見事敵をやぶった。このことから繁長は出羽神社の神を分祀して当社に合祀し、当社を総鎮守とした。その後[[村上忠勝]]のとき、社地は西奈弥山頂上からその中腹へと移った。 [[本庄氏]]から[[堀氏]]へと統治者が変っても同じく崇敬された。[[堀直寄]]は[[村上城]]の改築を行い、三層の天守が作られたが、天守の上からは当社を見下ろすことになってしまい恐れ多いとして、[[寛永]]10年（[[1632年]]）、現在地に社殿を造営して遷座した。このときより当社は名を羽黒三所権現と変えた。遷座の日は旧暦6月7日で、以降この日が[[例祭]]日とされ、明治の太陽暦採用以後は月遅れの7月7日とされた。 [[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）に現在の名称に改称され、明治27年（1894年）郷社に、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）には県社に列した。 == 主な年中行事 == * [[7月7日]] - [[村上大祭]] == 現地情報 == 村上市街南部、山居山の中腹に立地する。 ; 交通アクセス * 村上市まちなか循環バス 「羽黒町」バス停より徒歩約3分 == 外部リンク == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 西奈彌羽黒神社（せなみはぐろじんじゃ）] - 村上市観光協会 * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 にいがた観光ナビ：西奈彌羽黒神社] - 新潟県観光協会 * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html 『羽黒神社とその祭礼』テキスト版] - 岩船広域図書館 - WayBack Machineによるアーカイブ {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せなみはくろしんしや}} [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧県社]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:村上市の歴史]] [[Category:羽黒神社]] [[Category:村上市の建築物]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: I realize that doing a recent edit on these ones is better than all of that other crap of manual cat a lot stuff
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]]

Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
