The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]], [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]][[en:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}}

During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]}} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[sn:E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}}

In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}}

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|qq=|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|qq=|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|qq=|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|qq=|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|qq=|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}}

Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}}

Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)・{{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Established = First year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Murakami, Niigata|Murakami, Niigata]], [[:en:Niigata Prefecture|Niigata Prefecture]]. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]]. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the grandson of [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), [[:en:Honjō Shigenaga|Honjō Shigenaga]], who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daibōji clan|1=Daihōji clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434351}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the [[:en:Dewa Shrine|Dewa Shrine]] and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}}

Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Tadakatsu|1=Murakami Tadakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522794}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori clan|1=Hori clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427499}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Naomune|1=Hori Naoyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427694}}, who renovated [[:en:Murakami Castle|Murakami Castle]], built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Grand Festival|1=Murakami Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522760}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈弥羽黒神社]] {{translated page|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|version=96441671|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)・{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = First year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), {{ill|Honjō Shigenaga|ja|本庄繁長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the {{ill|Mogami clan|ja|最上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the {{ill|Dewa Shrine|ja|出羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]]

Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), {{ill|Honjō Shigenaga|ja|本庄繁長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the {{ill|Mogami clan|ja|最上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the {{ill|Dewa Shrine|ja|出羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

'''Senami Haguro Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Senami Haguro Shrine|lt=Senamihaguro Shrine|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|WD=Q11627587}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Nishinamino Shrine|lt=Nishinamino Shrine|WD=Q135040474}} || {{coord|38.217522|139.478242|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182467 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)・{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = First year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), {{ill|Honjō Shigenaga|ja|本庄繁長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the {{ill|Mogami clan|ja|最上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the {{ill|Dewa Shrine|ja|出羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
'''Senami Haguro Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Senami Haguro Shrine|lt=Senamihaguro Shrine|ja|西奈弥羽黒神社|WD=Q11627587}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Nishinamino Shrine|lt=Nishinamino Shrine|WD=Q135040474}} || {{coord|38.217522|139.478242|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182467 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11627587}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)・{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = First year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), {{ill|Honjō Shigenaga|ja|本庄繁長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the {{ill|Mogami clan|ja|最上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the {{ill|Dewa Shrine|ja|出羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)・{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = First year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Senami Haguro Shrine | image = [[File:Senami-Haguro Jinja Shrine, Murakami, Niigata, Japan, October 2021.jpg|250px|Senami Haguro Shrine]]{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=13|marker=building}} | location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Hagurocho 6-16 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|13|3.08|N|139|28|41.67|E|scale:10000}} | deity = Natsu-hime no Ōkami
Ukanomitama no Mikoto
Tsukuyomi no Mikoto | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)・{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established = First year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) | festival = July 7 }} '''Senami Haguro Shrine''' (西奈彌羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Murakami, Niigata|ja|村上市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|ja|新潟県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along with {{ill|Senami Shrine|ja|西奈彌神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Senami Hamamachi, it is considered a possible location of the "Senami Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a shrine of {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国磐船郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Locally, it is simply called '''Haguro Shrine'''. The original deity enshrined was Natsu-hime no Ōkami, and later, Ukanomitama no Mikoto and Tsukuyomi no Mikoto were added from {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the Edo period, the shrine was known as "Haguro Sansho Gongen." Natsu-hime no Kami is believed to be the daughter of {{ill|Hayamato|ja|羽戸山命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the grandson of {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is considered the deity who developed the area. == History == The shrine was established in the first year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (687 AD) and was originally located at the summit of Mount Senami. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), {{ill|Honjō Shigenaga|ja|本庄繁長|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled the area, went to the aid of the {{ill|Daibōji clan|ja|大宝寺氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Dewa Shōnai, leading to conflict with the {{ill|Mogami clan|ja|最上氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tōzenji clan (vassals of the Daibōji). Shigenaga prayed at the {{ill|Dewa Shrine|ja|出羽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and, after his victory, enshrined part of its deity in Senami Haguro Shrine. The shrine then became the region’s main shrine. Later, under the rule of {{ill|Murakami Tadakatsu|ja|村上忠勝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was relocated from the summit to its current location midway up Mount Senami. Despite the transition from the Honjō to the {{ill|Hori clan|ja|堀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine continued to receive veneration. {{ill|Hori Naomune|ja|堀直寄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who renovated {{ill|Murakami Castle|ja|村上城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built a three-story keep that overlooked the shrine. Finding this disrespectful, he ordered the shrine relocated to its present site in 1632 (Kan'ei 10). At this time, the shrine adopted the name "Haguro Sansho Gongen." The relocation date was recorded as the 7th day of the 6th lunar month, which became the annual festival date. After Japan adopted the solar calendar in the Meiji era, the festival was shifted to July 7. In 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In 1894 (Meiji 27), it was designated a Gōsha (village shrine), and in 1928 (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Major Annual Events == * {{ill|Murakami Grand Festival|ja|村上大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (July 7) == location and Access == Senami Haguro Shrine is located in the southern part of Murakami city, midway up Mount Sankyo. ; Transportation * 3-minute walk from the "Hagurocho" bus stop on the Murakami City Loop Bus. == External Links == * [https://www.sake3.com/spot/54 Senami Haguro Shrine] - Murakami City Tourism Association * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/7176 Niigata Sightseeing Navi: Senami Haguro Shrine] - Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071024101757/http://www.iwafune.ed.jp/t/kyoudo/t.haguro1.html "Haguro Shrine and its Festivals" (Archived)] - Iwafune Regional Library via Wayback Machine {{translated page|ja|西奈彌羽黒神社|version=7275903|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Murakami, Niigata]] [[Category:Sinami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:西奈彌羽黒神社]] [[ja:%E8%A5%BF%E5%A5%88%E5%BC%A5%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Senami Haguro Shrine]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
